metadata.title: New Zealand Information Security Manual (NZISM) v3.9

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: System owners seeking a dispensation for non-compliance with any baseline controls in the NZISM MUST be granted a dispensation by their Accreditation Authority. Where High Assurance Cryptographic Systems (HACS) are implemented, the Accreditation Authority will be the Director-General GCSB or a formal delegate.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: If a system processes, stores or communicates classified information from another agency, that agency MUST be consulted before a decision to be non-compliant with the NZ Government Security Classification System is made.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If a system processes, stores or communicates classified information from a foreign government, that government MUST be consulted before a decision to be non-compliant with NZISM controls is made.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If a system processes, stores or communicates data and information with multiple agencies or forms part of an All-of-Government system, interested parties MUST be formally consulted before non-compliance with any baseline controls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD review decisions to be non-compliant with any controls at least annually.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST retain a copy and maintain a record of the supporting risk assessment and decisions to be non-compliant with any baseline controls from the NZISM.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where good and recommended practice controls are NOT implemented, agencies MUST record and formally recognise that non-use of any controls without due consideration may increase residual risk for the agency. This residual risk MUST be agreed and acknowledged by the Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies undertaking system design activities for in-house or out-sourced projects MUST use the latest version of the NZISM for information security requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When GCSB makes a determination that newly introduced standard, policy or guideline within the NZISM, or any additional information security policy, is of particular importance, agencies MUST comply with any new specified requirements and implementation timeframes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security personnel MUST familiarise themselves with the information security roles and services provided by New Zealand Government organisations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies engaging industry for the provision of off-site information technology services and functions MUST accredit the systems used by the contractor to at least the same minimum standard as the agency’s systems. This may be achieved through a third party review report utilising the ISAE 3402 Assurance Reports on Controls at a Third Party Service Organisation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD NOT engage industry for the provision of off-site information technology services and functions in countries that New Zealand does not have a multilateral or bilateral security agreement with for the protection of classified information of the government of New Zealand. If there is any doubt, the agency’s CISO should be consulted.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where an agency has outsourced information technology services and functions, any ITSMs within the agency SHOULD be independent of the company providing the information technology services and functions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where an agency has outsourced information technology services and functions, they SHOULD ensure that the outsourced organisation provides a single point of contact within the organisation for all information assurance and security matters.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD develop a program to manage contractors that have been accredited for the provision of off-site information technology services and functions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt public cloud technologies or services MUST develop a plan for how they intend to use these services.  This plan can be standalone or part of an overarching ICT strategy.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt public cloud technologies or services SHOULD incorporate Zero Trust philosophies and concepts.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD leverage public cloud environment native security services as part of legacy system migrations, in preference to recreating application architectures that rely on legacy perimeter controls for security.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt cloud technologies or services MUST conduct a comprehensive risk assessment, in accordance with the guidance provided by the Government Chief Digital Officer (GCDO) before implementation or adoption.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure cloud risks for any cloud service adopted are identified, understood and formally accepted by the Agency Head or Chief Executive and the agency’s Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt cloud services SHOULD review and enhance existing security architectures and systems design to prudently manage the changed risk, technology and security environment in adopting cloud services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST consider the use of any All of Government contracts with cloud service providers before negotiating individual contracts.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agency system architectures and supply arrangements and contracts SHOULD include provision for the safe return of agency data in the event of system or service termination or contract termination.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure they are aware of the latest developments in post-quantum cryptography.  GCSB is tracking these developments and will continue to provide advice through the NZISM.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD maintain an inventory of sensitive and critical datasets that must be secured for an extended amount of time.  This will ensure datasets that may be at risk now and decrypted once a cryptographically relevant quantum computer is available are not secured solely through the use of quantum vulnerable cryptography.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD conduct an inventory of systems using cryptographic technologies to determine the potential size and scope of future transition work once post-quantum cryptographic systems become available.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD identify which systems in their inventory rely on public key cryptography and note them as quantum vulnerable in agency risk assessments.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD determine a priority order for quantum vulnerable systems to be transitioned from classical cryptography to post-quantum cryptography.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD consider the following factors when prioritising the quantum vulnerable systems:

Is the system a high value asset based on agency requirements?
Does the system protect sensitive information (e.g., key stores, passwords, root keys, signing keys, personal information, and classified information)?
Do other systems (internal or external to the agency) depend on the cryptographic protections in place on the quantum vulnerable system?
How long does the data need to be protected?

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Using the inventory and prioritisation information, agencies SHOULD develop a plan for system transitions upon publication of the new post-quantum cryptographic standard.

groups.title: 3. Information security governance - roles and responsibilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where the agency head devolves their authority the delegate MUST be at least a member of the Senior Executive Team or an equivalent management position.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When the agency head delegates their authority, the delegate SHOULD be a senior executive who understands the consequences and potential impact to the business of the acceptance of residual risk.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where the head of a smaller agency is not able to satisfy all segregation of duty requirements because of scalability and small personnel numbers, all potential conflicts of interest SHOULD be clearly identified, declared and actively managed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The agency head MUST provide support for the development, implementation and ongoing maintenance of information security processes within their agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
The CISO MUST be:

cleared for access to all classified information processed by the agency’s systems, and
able to be briefed into any compartmented information on the agency’s systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD appoint a person to the role of CISO or have the role undertaken by an existing person within the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO role SHOULD be undertaken by a member of the Senior Executive Team or an equivalent management position.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD be responsible for overseeing the management of security personnel within the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where multiple roles are held by the CISO any potential conflicts of interest SHOULD be identified and carefully managed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD report directly to the agency head on matters of information security within the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD develop and maintain a comprehensive strategic level information security and security risk management program within the agency aimed at protecting the agency’s official and classified information.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD be responsible for the development of an information security communications plan.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD create and facilitate the agency security risk management process.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD work with system owners, system certifiers and system accreditors to determine appropriate information security policies for their systems and ensure consistency with the Protective Security Requirements (PSR) and in particular the relevant NZISM components.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD be responsible for establishing mechanisms and programs to ensure compliance with the information security policies and standards within the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD be responsible for ensuring agency compliance with the NZISM through facilitating a continuous program of certification and accreditation of all agency systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD be responsible for the implementation of information security measurement metrics and key performance indicators within the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD facilitate information security and business alignment and communication through an information security steering committee or advisory board which meets formally and on a regular basis, and comprises key business and ICT executives.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD be responsible for coordinating information security and security risk management projects between business and information security teams.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD work with business teams to facilitate security risk analysis and security risk management processes, including the identification of acceptable levels of risk consistently across the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD provide strategic level guidance for agency ICT projects and operations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD liaise with agency technology architecture teams to ensure alignment between security and agency architectures.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD coordinate the use of external information security resources to the agency including contracting and managing the resources.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD be responsible for controlling the information security budget.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD be fully aware of all information security incidents within the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD coordinate the development of disaster recovery policies and standards within the agency to ensure that business-critical services are supported appropriately and that information security is maintained in the event of a disaster.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD be responsible for overseeing the development and operation of information security awareness and training programs within the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The CISO SHOULD provide authoritative security advice and have familiarity with a range of national and international standards and good practice.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST appoint at least one ITSM within their agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
ITSMs MUST be:

cleared for access to all classified information processed by the agency’s systems; and
able to be briefed into any compartmented information on the agency’s systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where an agency is spread across a number of geographical sites, it is recommended that the agency SHOULD appoint a local ITSM at each major site.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The ITSM role SHOULD be undertaken by personnel with an appropriate level of authority and training based on the size of the agency or their area of responsibility within the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD NOT have additional responsibilities beyond those needed to fulfil the role as outlined within this manual.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD work with the CISO to develop an information security program within the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD undertake and manage projects to address identified security risks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs MUST be responsible for assisting system owners to obtain and maintain the accreditation of their systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD identify systems that require security measures and assist in the selection of appropriate information security measures for such systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD consult with ICT project personnel to ensure that information security is included in the evaluation, selection, installation, configuration and operation of IT equipment and software.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD work with agency enterprise architecture teams to ensure that security risk assessments are incorporated into system architectures and to identify, evaluate and select information security solutions to meet the agency’s security objectives.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD be included in the agency’s change management and change control processes to ensure that risks are properly identified and controls are properly applied to manage those risks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD notify the Accreditation Authority of any significant change that may affect the accreditation of that system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD liaise with vendors and agency purchasing and legal areas to establish mutually acceptable information security contracts and service-level agreements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs MUST be responsible for ensuring the development, maintenance, updating and implementation of Security Risk Management Plans (SRMPs), Systems Security Plans (SSP) and any Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for all agency systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD conduct security risk assessments on the implementation of new or updated IT equipment or software in the existing environment and develop treatment strategies if necessary.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD select and coordinate the implementation of controls to support and enforce information security policies.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD provide leadership and direction for the integration of information security strategies and architecture with agency business and ICT strategies and architecture.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD provide technical and managerial expertise for the administration of information security management tools.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD work with the CISO to develop information security budget projections and resource allocations based on short-term and long-term goals and objectives.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD coordinate, measure and report on technical aspects of information security management.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD monitor and report on compliance with information security policies, as well as the enforcement of information security policies within the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD provide regular reports on information security incidents and other areas of particular concern to the CISO.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD assess and report on threats, vulnerabilities, and residual security risks and recommend remedial actions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD assist system owners and security personnel in understanding and responding to audit failures reported by auditors.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD assist and guide the disaster recovery planning team in the selection of recovery strategies and the development, testing and maintenance of disaster recovery plans.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD provide or arrange for the provision of information security awareness and training for all agency personnel.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD develop technical information materials and workshops on information security trends, threats, good practices and control mechanisms as appropriate.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD maintain a current and up-to-date security knowledge base comprising of a technical reference library, security advisories and alerts, information on information security trends and practices, and relevant laws, regulations, standards and guidelines.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD provide expert guidance on security matters for ICT projects.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs SHOULD provide technical advice for the information security steering committee, change management committee and other agency and inter-agency committees as required.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The ITSM SHOULD keep the CISO and system owners informed with up-to-date information on current threats.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Each system MUST have a system owner who is responsible for the operation and maintenance of the system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: System owners SHOULD be a member of the Senior Executive Team or an equivalent management position, for large or critical agency systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: System owners MUST obtain and maintain accreditation of their system(s).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: System owners MUST ensure the development, maintenance and implementation of complete, accurate and up to date Information Security documentation for systems under their ownership. Such actions MUST be documented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: System Owners MUST involve the ITSM in the redevelopment and updates of the Information Security documentation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All system users MUST comply with the relevant security policies and procedures for the systems they use.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: System users that need to bypass security policies, procedures or mechanisms for any reason MUST seek formal authorisation from the CISO or the ITSM if this authority has been specifically delegated to the ITSM.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All systems MUST undergo an audit as part of the certification process.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The certification authority MUST accept that the controls are appropriate, effective and comply with the Protective Security Requirements (PSR) and in particular the relevant NZISM components, in order to award certification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Following the audit, the certification authority SHOULD produce an assessment for the Accreditation Authority outlining the residual security risks relating to the operation of the system and a recommendation on whether to award accreditation or not.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that auditors conducting audits are able to demonstrate independence and are not also the system owner or certification authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Prior to undertaking the audit the system owner MUST approve the system architecture and associated information security documentation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The SecPol, SRMP, SSP, SOPs and IRP documentation MUST be reviewed by the auditor to ensure that it is comprehensive and appropriate for the environment the system is to operate within.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Information Security Policy (SecPol) MUST be reviewed by the auditor to ensure that all applicable controls specified in this manual are addressed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The system and security architecture (including information security documentation) SHOULD be reviewed by the auditor to ensure that it is based on sound information security principles and meets information security requirements, including the NZISM.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Information Security Policy (SecPol) SHOULD be reviewed by the auditor to ensure that policies have been developed or identified by the agency to protect classified information that is processed, stored or communicated by its systems.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Prior to undertaking any system testing in support of the certification process, the system owner MUST implement the controls for the system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The implementation of controls MUST be assessed to determine whether they have been implemented correctly and are operating effectively.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The auditor MUST ensure that, where applicable, a physical security certification has been awarded by an appropriate physical security certification authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The physical security certification SHOULD be less than three (3) years old at the time of the audit.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The auditor MUST produce a report of compliance for the certification authority outlining areas of non-compliance for a system and any suggested remediation actions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop an accreditation framework for their agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that each of their systems is awarded accreditation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that all systems are awarded accreditation before they are used operationally.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that all systems are awarded accreditation prior to connecting them to any other internal or external system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure information security monitoring, logging and auditing is conducted on all accredited systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: For multi-national and multi-agency systems, the Certification and Accreditation Authorities SHOULD be determined by a formal agreement between the parties involved.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Prior to beginning the accreditation process the system owner SHOULD advise the certification and accreditation authorities of their intent to seek certification and accreditation for their system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD confirm governance arrangements with the certification authorities, and with the accreditation authorities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where an agency’s system exchanges information with a third-party system, the agency MUST ensure that the receiving party has appropriate measures in place to provide a level of protection commensurate with the classification or privacy requirements of their information and that the third party is authorised to receive that information.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: An agency MUST ensure that a third party complies with the agency’s information security expectations through a formal process providing assurance to agency management that the operation of information security within the third party meets, and continues to meet, these expectations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD review accreditation deliverables when determining whether the receiving party has appropriate measures in place to provide a level of protection commensurate with the classification of their information.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT allow a system to process, store or communicate classified information above the classification for which the system has received accreditation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A system that processes, stores or communicates compartmented information MUST be accredited by the GCSB.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that systems processing, storing or communicating NZEO information remain under the control of a New Zealand national working for the New Zealand government, at all times.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that the period between accreditations of each of their systems does not exceed three years.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST notify associated agencies where multiple agencies are connected to agency systems operating with expired accreditations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST notify the Government Chief Digital Officer (GCDO) where All-of-Government systems are connected to agency systems operating with expired accreditations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT operate a system without accreditation or with a lapsed accreditation unless the accreditation authority has granted a dispensation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that the period between accreditations of each of their systems does not exceed two years.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All systems MUST be certified as part of the accreditation process.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Accreditation Authority MUST accept the residual security risk relating to the operation of a system in order to award accreditation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Accreditation Authority MUST advise other agencies where the accreditation decision may affect those agencies.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Accreditation Authority MUST advise the GCDO where the accreditation decision may affect any All-of-Government systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST have a SecPol for their agency. The SecPol is usually sponsored by the Chief Executive and managed by the CISO or Chief Information Officer (CIO). The ITSM should be the custodian of the SecPol. The SecPol should include an acceptable use policy for any agency technology equipment, systems, resources and data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All systems MUST have a documented Systems Architecture.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that every system is covered by a Security Risk Management Plan, which includes identification of risk owners.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that every system is covered by a SSP.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) are developed for systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop an Incident Response Plan and supporting procedures.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agency personnel MUST be trained in and periodically exercise the Incident Response Plan.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD document procedures relating to securing classified information and systems when required to evacuate a facility in the event of an emergency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that information security documentation is developed by personnel with a good understanding of policy requirements, the subject matter, essential processes and the agency’s business and operations

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that their SRMP, Systems Architecture, SSP, SOPs and IRP are logically connected and consistent for each system, other agency systems and with the agency’s SecPol.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD create and maintain an overarching document describing the agency’s documentation framework, including a complete listing of all information security documentation that shows a document hierarchy and defines how each document is related to the other.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where an agency lacks an existing, well-defined documentation framework, they SHOULD use the document names defined in this manual.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where an agency uses alternative documentation names to those defined within this manual for their information security documentation they SHOULD convert the documentation names to those used in this manual.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that their SecPol, SRMP, SSP, SOPs and IRP are appropriately classified.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
When information security documentation development is outsourced, agencies SHOULD:

review the documents for suitability;
retain control over the content; and
ensure that all policy requirements are met.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All information security documentation SHOULD be formally approved and signed off by a person with an appropriate level of seniority and authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD ensure that:

all high-level information security documentation is approved by the CISO and the agency head or their delegate; and
all system-specific documents are reviewed by the ITSM and approved by the system owner.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD develop a regular schedule for reviewing all information security documentation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD ensure that information security documentation is reviewed:

at least annually; or
in response to significant changes in the environment, business or system; and
with the date of the most recent review being recorded on each document.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Information Security Policy (SecPol) SHOULD document the information security guidelines, standards and responsibilities of an agency.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD determine agency and system specific security risks that could warrant additional controls to those specified in this manual.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Security Risk Management Plan SHOULD contain a security risk assessment and a corresponding treatment strategy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD incorporate their SRMP into their wider agency risk management plan.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD develop their SRMP in accordance with international standards for risk management.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST select controls from this manual to be included in the SSP based on the scope of the system with additional system specific controls being included as a result of the associated SRMP. Encryption Key Management requires specific consideration; refer to Chapter 17 – Cryptography.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use the latest baseline of this manual when developing, and updating, their SSPs as part of the certification, accreditation and reaccreditation of their systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD include a Key Management Plan in the SSP.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD develop SOPs for each of the following roles:

ITSM;
system administrator; and
system user.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
The following procedures SHOULD be documented in the ITSMs SOPs.

Topic
Procedures to be included

Access control
Authorising access rights to applications and data.

Asset Musters
Labelling, registering and mustering assets, including media.

Audit logs
Reviewing system audit trails and manual logs, particularly for privileged users.

Configuration control
Approving and releasing changes to the system software or configurations.

Information security incidents
Detecting, reporting and managing potential information security incidents.

 
Establishing the cause of any information security incident, whether accidental or deliberate.

 
Actions to be taken to recover and minimise the exposure from an information security incident.

 
Additional actions to prevent reoccurrence.

Data transfers
Managing the review of media containing classified information that is to be transferred off-site.

 
Managing the review of incoming media for malware or unapproved software.

IT equipment
Managing the disposal & destruction of unserviceable IT equipment and media.

System Patching
Advising and recommending system patches, updates and version changes based on security notices and related advisories.

System integrity audit
Reviewing system user accounts, system parameters and access controls to ensure that the system is secure.

 
Checking the integrity of system software.

 
Testing access controls.

System maintenance

Managing the ongoing security and functionality of system software, including: maintaining awareness of current software vulnerabilities, testing and applying software patches/updates/signatures, and applying appropriate hardening techniques.

User account management
Authorising new system users.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
The following procedures SHOULD be documented in the system administrator’s SOPs.

 

Topic
Procedures to be included

Access control
Implementing access rights to applications and data.

Configuration control
Implementing changes to the system software or configurations.

System backup and recovery
Backing up data, including audit logs.

 
Securing backup tapes.

 
Recovering from system failures.

User account management
Adding and removing system users.

 
Setting system user privileges.

 
Cleaning up directories and files when a system user departs or changes roles.

Incident response
Detecting, reporting and managing potential information security incidents.

 
Establishing the cause of any information security incident, whether accidental or deliberate.

 
Actions to be taken to recover and minimise the exposure from information security incident.

 
Additional actions to prevent reoccurrence.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
The following procedures SHOULD be documented in the system user’s SOPs.

Topic

Procedures to be included

Acceptable Use 
Acceptable uses of the system(s).

End of day
How to secure systems at the end of the day.

Information security incidents

What to do in the case of a suspected or actual information security incident.

Media control
Procedures for handling and using media.

Passwords

Choosing and protecting passwords.

Temporary absence
How to secure systems when temporarily absent.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: ITSMs, system administrators and system users SHOULD sign a statement that they have read and agree to abide by their respective SOPs.

parts.name: statement
statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST include in procedures for personnel evacuating a facility the requirement to secure classified information and systems prior to the evacuation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST conduct a risk assessment in order to determine the type and level of independent assurance required to satisfy certification and accreditation requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In all cases where assurance on service provider operations cannot be obtained directly, agencies SHOULD obtain independent assurance reports.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In order to address identified risk areas, agencies SHOULD obtain relevant assurance reports and service provider certifications to inform a risk assessment and Certification activities as well as other aspects of the certification processes such as evidence of controls effectiveness and remediation plans.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST incorporate the results of any independent assurance reports into the agency Certification process, to understand the residual risk position and controls required to manage risk appropriately.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An agency MUST undertake a risk assessment to determine which systems and services to include in the agency’s VDP.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An agency MUST develop and publish a VDP.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: An agency SHOULD publish a security.txt to permit secure communications and direct any reports to a specific agency resource, in accordance with the agency’s VDP.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An agency MUST commit to addressing disclosed vulnerabilities within the timeframe it sets in its policy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An agency’s vulnerability disclosure timeframe SHOULD be set to no more than 90 days.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure they integrate their VDP with other elements of their information security policies.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD undertake and document information security reviews of their systems at least annually.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD have information security reviews conducted by personnel independent to the target of the review or by an independent third party.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD review the components detailed in the table below. Agencies SHOULD also ensure that any adjustments and changes as a result of any vulnerability analysis are consistent with the vulnerability disclosure policy.

Component
Review

Information security documentation
The SecPol, Systems Architecture, SRMPs, SSPs, SitePlan, SOPs, the VDP, the IRP, and any third party assurance reports.

Dispensations
Prior to the identified expiry date.

Operating environment
When an identified threat emerges or changes, an agency gains or loses a function or the operation of functions are moved to a new physical environment.

Procedures
After an information security incident or test exercise.

System security
Items that could affect the security of the system on a regular basis.

Threats
Changes in threat environment and risk profile.

NZISM
Changes to baseline or other controls, any new controls and guidance.

parts.name: statement
statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD analyse and treat all vulnerabilities and subsequent security risks to their systems identified during a vulnerability assessment.

parts.name: statement
statement
statement
parts.prose: An agency’s change management process MUST define appropriate actions to be followed before and after urgent changes are implemented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An agency’s change management process SHOULD define appropriate actions to be followed before and after urgent changes are implemented.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: When a configuration change impacts the security of a system and is subsequently assessed as having changed the overall security risk for the system, the agency MUST reaccredit the system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST determine availability and recovery requirements for their systems and implement measures consistent with the agency's SRMP to support them.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD develop and document a business continuity plan.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD develop and document a disaster recovery plan.

parts.name: statement
statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use the results of the security risk assessment to determine the appropriate balance of resources allocated to prevention versus resources allocated to detection of information security incidents.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST direct personnel to report information security incidents to an ITSM as soon as possible after the information security incident is discovered in accordance with agency procedures.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: The ITSM MUST keep the CISO fully informed of information security incidents within an agency.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD report information security incidents categorised as Low to the NCSC.

A Report Form is provided on the NCSC website under Reporting an Incident at Report an incident and request support | National Cyber Security Centre

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD formally share post-incident review reports by emailing them to incidents@ncsc.govt.nz.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies that outsource their information technology services and functions MUST ensure that the service provider advises and consults with the agency when an information security incident occurs.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST notify all system users of any suspected or confirmed loss or compromise of keying material.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST replace compromised cryptographic keys as a matter of urgency and record the replacement in the incident reporting.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST urgently notify GCSB of any suspected loss or compromise of keying material associated with HACE.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST detail information security incident responsibilities and procedures for each system in the relevant Information Security Documents.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that all information security incidents are recorded in a register.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use their incidents register as a reference for future security risk assessments.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST implement procedures and processes to detect data spills or data breach.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When a data spill occurs agencies MUST assume that data at the highest classification held on or processed by the system, has been compromised.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agency SOPs MUST include procedure for:

all personnel with access to systems; 
notification to the ITSM of any data spillage or breaches; and
notification to the ITSM of access to any data which they are not authorised to access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST document procedures for dealing with data spills or data breaches in their IRP.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST treat any data spill or data breach as an information security incident and follow the IRP to deal with it.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When a data spill or data breach occurs agencies MUST report the details to the Privacy Commissioner and information owner in accordance with the Privacy Act 2020.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: When classified information is introduced onto a system not accredited to handle the information, personnel MUST NOT copy, view, print or email the information.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When a data spill or data breach involving classified or sensitive information or contamination of classified systems occurs and systems cannot be segregated, or isolated agencies SHOULD immediately contact the NCSC for further advice.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies considering allowing an attacker to continue some actions under controlled conditions for the purpose of seeking further information or evidence MUST seek legal advice.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD:

transfer a copy of raw audit trails and other relevant data onto media for secure archiving, as well as securing manual log records for retention; and
ensure that all personnel involved in the investigation maintain a record of actions undertaken to support the investigation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that any requests for NCSC assistance are made as soon as possible after the information security incident is detected and that no actions which could affect the integrity of the evidence are carried out prior to NCSC’s involvement.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that any facility containing a system or its associated infrastructure, including deployable systems, are certified and accredited in accordance with the PSR.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD prevent unauthorised people from observing systems, in particular desks, screens and keyboards.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD position desks, screens and keyboards away from windows and doorways so that they cannot be overseen by unauthorised persons.  If required, blinds or drapes SHOULD be fixed to the inside of windows, and doors kept closed to avoid oversight.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT permit non-agency owned devices to be brought into TOP SECRET areas without prior approval from the Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that technical surveillance counter-measure tests are conducted as a part of the physical security certification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST determine if further technical surveillance counter-measure testing is required, particularly if visitors or contractors have entered secure areas.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that servers and network devices are secured within cabinets as outlined in PSR Management protocol for physical security, with supporting document – Storage requirements for electronic information in ICT facilities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that keys or equivalent access mechanisms to server rooms, communications rooms and security containers are appropriately controlled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT leave server rooms, communications rooms or security containers in an unsecured state unless the server room is occupied by authorised personnel.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies operating no-lone-zones MUST suitably signpost the area and have all entry and exit points appropriately secured.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST certify the physical security of facilities containing network infrastructure to the highest classification of information being communicated over the network infrastructure.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies communicating classified information over infrastructure in secure areas SHOULD encrypt their information with at least an Approved Cryptographic Protocol. See Section 17.3 – Approved Cryptographic Protocols.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST locate patch panels, fibre distribution panels and structured wiring enclosures within at least lockable commercial cabinets.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD locate patch panels, fibre distribution panels and structured wiring enclosures within at least lockable commercial cabinets.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies communicating classified information over public network infrastructure or over infrastructure in unsecure areas MUST use encryption to lower the handling instructions to be equivalent to those for unclassified networks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST account for all IT equipment containing media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST certify the physical security of facilities containing IT equipment to the highest classification of information being processed, stored or communicated by the equipment within the facilities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that when secure areas are non-operational or when work areas are unoccupied IT equipment with media is secured in accordance with the minimum physical security requirements for storing classified information as specified in the PSR Management protocol for physical security with supporting document – Storage requirements for electronic information in ICT facilities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies choosing to prevent the storage of classified information on non-volatile media and enforcing scrubbing of temporary data at logoff or shutdown SHOULD:

assess the security risks associated with such a decision; and
specify the processes and conditions for their application within the system’s SSP.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies securing volatile media for IT equipment during non-operational hours SHOULD:

disconnect power from the equipment the media resides within;
assess the security risks if not sanitising the media; and
specify any additional processes and controls that will be applied within the system’s SSP.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD encrypt media within IT equipment with an Approved Cryptographic Algorithm. See Section 17.2 - Approved Cryptographic Algorithms.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST record the usage of seals in a register that is appropriately secured.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST record in a register, information on:

issue and usage details of seals and associated tools;
serial numbers of all seals purchased; and
the location or asset on which each seal is used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD record the usage of seals in a register that is appropriately secured.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD record in a register information on:

issue and usage details of seals and associated tools;
serial numbers of all seals purchased; and
the location or asset on which each seal is used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consult with the seal manufacturer to ensure that, if available, any purchased seals and sealing tools display a unique identifier or image appropriate to the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Seals and any seal application tools SHOULD be secured when not in use.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD NOT allow contractors to independently purchase seals and associated tools on behalf of the government.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD review seals for differences with a register at least annually. At the same time seals SHOULD be examined for any evidence of tampering.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agency management MUST ensure that all personnel who have access to a system have sufficient training and ongoing information security awareness.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST provide ongoing information security awareness and a training programme for personnel on topics such as responsibilities, legislation and regulation, consequences of non-compliance with information security policies and procedures, and potential security risks and counter-measures.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST provide information security awareness training as part of their employee induction programmes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD align the detail, content and coverage of information security awareness and training programmes to system user responsibilities.

parts.name: statement
statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST provide all system users with familiarisation training on the information security policies and procedures and the secure operation of the system before being granted unsupervised access to the system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD advise personnel attending courses along with non-government personnel not to disclose any details that could be used to compromise agency security.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST specify in the System Security Plan (SSP) any authorisations, security clearances and briefings necessary for system access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST:

limit system access on a need-to-know/need-to-access basis;
provide system users with the least amount of privileges needed to undertake their duties; and
have any requests for access to a system authorised by the supervisor or manager of the system user.

parts.name: statement
statement
statement
parts.prose: System users MUST NOT be granted access to systems or information classified CONFIDENTIAL or above unless vetting procedures have been completed and formal security clearance granted.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
All system users MUST:

hold a security clearance or other authorisation appropriate for the system classification; or
have been granted access in accordance with the requirements in the PSR for emergency access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All system users MUST have received any necessary briefings before being granted access to compartmented or endorsed information or systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where systems process, store or communicate unprotected NZEO information, agencies MUST NOT allow foreign nationals, including seconded foreign nationals, to have access to the system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where agencies protect NZEO information on a system by implementing controls to ensure that NZEO information is not passed to, or made accessible to, foreign nationals, agencies MUST NOT allow foreign nationals, including seconded foreign nationals, to have access to the system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where systems process, store or communicate classified information with nationality releasability markings, agencies MUST NOT allow foreign nationals, including seconded foreign nationals, to have access to such information that is not marked as releasable to their nation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT permit limited higher access for systems and information classified CONFIDENTIAL or above.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose:
Agencies granting limited higher access to a system MUST ensure that:

the approval for access is formally acknowledged and recorded; and either

effective controls are in place to restrict access only to classified information that is necessary to undertake the system user’s duties; or
the system user is continually supervised by another system user who has the appropriate security clearances to access the system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Emergency access MUST NOT be granted unless personnel have a security clearance to at least CONFIDENTIAL level.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Emergency access MUST NOT be used on reassignment of duties while awaiting completion of full security clearance procedures.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Personnel granted emergency access MUST be debriefed at the conclusion of the emergency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT grant limited higher access to systems that process, store or communicate endorsed or compartmented information.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure personnel are instructed to report any suspicious activity, questioning or contact when using the Internet, to an ITSM.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST make their system users aware of the agency’s Web usage policies.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel MUST formally acknowledge and accept agency Web usage policies.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD implement measures to monitor their personnel, visitor and contractor compliance with their Web usage policies.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure personnel are instructed to take special care when posting information on the Web.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure personnel posting information on the Web maintain separate professional accounts from any personal accounts they have for websites.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD monitor websites where personnel post information and if necessary remove or request the removal of any inappropriate information.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Accessing personal accounts from agency systems SHOULD be discouraged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that personnel are informed of the security risks associated with posting personal information on websites, especially for those personnel holding higher level security clearances.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel SHOULD be encouraged to use privacy settings for websites to restrict access to personal information they post to only those they authorise to view it.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel SHOULD be encouraged to undertake a Web search of themselves to determine what personal information is available and contact an ITSM if they need assistance in determining if the information is appropriate to be viewed by the general public or potential adversaries.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD NOT allow personnel to use peer-to-peer applications over the Internet.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD NOT allow personnel to receive files via peer-to-peer, IM or IRC applications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that all personnel with unescorted access to TOP SECRET areas have appropriate security clearances and briefings.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST maintain an up to date list of personnel entitled to enter a TOP SECRET area without an escort.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel MUST display identity cards at all times while within the secure area.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD display within a TOP SECRET area, an up to date list of personnel entitled to enter the area without an escort.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The unescorted access list SHOULD NOT be visible from outside of the secure area.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Visitors SHOULD be carefully supervised to ensure they do not gain access to or have oversight of information above the level of their clearance or outside of their need-to-know.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT permit personnel not on the unescorted access list to enter a TOP SECRET area unless their visit is recorded in a visitor log and they are escorted by a person on the unescorted access list.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies with a TOP SECRET area within a larger facility MUST maintain a separate log from any general visitor log.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST use fibre optic cable for backbone infrastructures and installations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use fibre optic cable for backbone infrastructures and installations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use fibre optic cabling.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consult with the GCSB where fibre optic cable incorporating conductive metal strengtheners or sheaths is specified.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consult with the GCSB where copper cables are specified.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD NOT use fibre optic cable incorporating conductive metal strengtheners or sheaths except where essential for cable integrity.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST install all cabling in accordance with the relevant New Zealand standards as directed by AS/NZS 3000:2007 and NZCSS400.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST comply with the cable and conduit colours specified in the following table.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Additional colours may be used to delineate special networks and compartmented information of the same classification. These networks MUST be labelled and covered in the agency’s SOPs.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The cable colour to be used for foreign systems MUST be agreed between the host agency, the foreign system owner and the Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT allow cable colours for foreign systems installed in New Zealand facilities to be the same colour as cables used for New Zealand systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The cable colour to be used for foreign systems SHOULD be agreed between the host agency, the foreign system owner and the Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD NOT allow cable colours for foreign systems installed in New Zealand facilities to be the same colour as cables used for New Zealand systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST contact GCSB for advice when combining the cabling of special networks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST NOT deviate from the approved fibre cable combinations for shared conduits and reticulation systems as indicated below.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: With fibre optic cables the arrangements of fibres within the cable sheath, as illustrated in Figure 3, MUST carry a single classification only.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If a fibre optic cable contains subunits, as shown in Figure 4, each subunit MUST carry only a single classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT mix classifications up to RESTRICTED with classifications of CONFIDENTIAL and above in a single cable.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When penetrating an audio secure area for cables, agencies MUST comply with all directions provided by GCSB.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cable groups sharing a wall outlet MUST use different connectors for systems of different classifications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In areas containing outlets for both TOP SECRET systems and systems of other classifications, agencies MUST ensure that the connectors for the TOP SECRET systems are different to those of the other systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cable outlets MUST be labelled with the system classification and connector type.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cable outlets SHOULD be labelled with the system classification and connector type.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Power filters SHOULD be used to provide a filtered power supply and reduce opportunity for technical attacks.

parts.prose: In TOP SECRET areas or zones, all cabling MUST be inspectable at a minimum of five-metre intervals.

controls.title: Cable Management for Non-Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cabling SHOULD be inspectable at a minimum of five-metre intervals.

controls.title: Cable Management for Non-Shared Government Facilities

parts.prose: Approved cable groups may share a common reticulation system but SHOULD have either a dividing partition or a visible gap between the differing cable groups or bundles.

controls.title: Cable Management for Non-Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Flexible or plastic conduit SHOULD be used in walls to run cabling from cable trays to wall outlets.

controls.title: Cable Management for Non-Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: TOP SECRET cabinets SHOULD have a visible inspectable gap between themselves and lower classified cabinets.

controls.title: Cable Management for Non-Shared Government Facilities

parts.prose: Power filters SHOULD be used to provide a filtered power supply and reduce opportunity for technical attacks.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use fibre optic cabling.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In TOP SECRET areas, cables MUST be fully inspectable for their entire length.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cabling SHOULD be inspectable at a minimum of five-metre intervals.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Approved cable groups SHOULD have either a dividing partition or a visible gap between the individual cable groups. If the partition or gap exists, cable groups may share a common reticulation system.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The front covers of conduits, ducts and cable trays in floors, ceilings and of associated fittings that contain TOP SECRET cabling, SHOULD be clear plastic.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cabling from cable trays to wall outlets SHOULD run in flexible or plastic conduit.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: For wall penetrations that exit into a lower classified area, cabling SHOULD be encased in conduit with all gaps between the conduit and the wall filled with an appropriate sealing compound.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: TOP SECRET facilities SHOULD have a power distribution board, separately reticulated, located within the TOP SECRET area and supply UPS power to all equipment.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Power filters SHOULD be used to provide a filtered power supply and reduce opportunity for technical attacks.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: TOP SECRET cabinets SHOULD have a visible gap to separate them from lower classified cabinets.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In TOP SECRET areas, agencies MUST use fibre optic cabling.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use fibre optic cabling.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: TOP SECRET cabling MUST run in a non-shared, enclosed reticulation system.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Approved cable groups can share a common reticulation system but SHOULD have either a dividing partition or a visible gap between the differing cable groups.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In TOP SECRET areas, the front covers for conduits and cable trays in floors, ceilings and of associated fittings MUST be clear plastic or be inspectable and have tamper proof seals fitted.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The front covers of conduits, ducts and cable trays in floors, ceilings and of associated fittings SHOULD be clear plastic or be inspectable and have tamper proof seals fitted.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Cabling MUST NOT run in a party wall.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST use GCSB endorsed tamper evident seals to seal all removable covers on reticulation systems, including:

conduit inspection boxes;
outlet and junction boxes; and
T-pieces.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Tamper evident seals MUST be uniquely identifiable and a register kept of their unique number and location.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Conduit joints MUST be sealed with glue or sealant.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Conduit joints SHOULD be sealed with glue or sealant.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Wall penetrations that exit into a lower classified area, cabling MUST be encased in conduit with all gaps between the conduit and the wall filled with an appropriate sealing compound.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Secure facilities MUST have a power distribution board located within the secure area and supply UPS power all equipment.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.prose: Power filters MUST be used to provide filtered (clean) power and reduce opportunity for technical attacks.

controls.title: Cable Management for Shared Non-Government Facilities

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Equipment cabinets MUST have a visible gap or non-conductive isolator between cabinets of different classifications.

parts.prose: There SHOULD be a visible inspectable gap or non-conductive isolator between equipment cabinets of different classifications.
