parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Conduit labels installed in public or visitor areas SHOULD NOT be labelled in such a way as to draw attention to or reveal classification of data processed or other agency capability.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Wall outlet boxes MUST denote the classification, cable and outlet numbers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Wall outlet boxes SHOULD denote the classification, cable and outlet numbers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The SOPs SHOULD record the site conventions for labelling and registration.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST label cables at each end, with sufficient information to enable the physical identification and inspection of the cable.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD label cables at each end, with sufficient information to enable the physical identification and inspection of the cable.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST maintain a register of cables.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD maintain a register of cables.

parts.name: statement
statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD inspect cables for inconsistencies with the cable register in accordance with the frequency defined in the SSP.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that only approved cable groups terminate on a patch panel.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: RED and BLACK cables must be separated and bundled.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In areas containing cabling for multiple classifications, agencies MUST ensure that the connectors for each classification are distinct and different to those of the other classifications.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In areas containing cabling for multiple classifications, agencies MUST document the selection of connector types for each classification.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In areas containing cabling for systems of different classifications, agencies SHOULD ensure that the connectors for each system are different to those of the other systems.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In areas containing cabling for systems of different classifications, agencies SHOULD document the selection of connector types.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD physically separate patch panels of different classifications by installing them in separate cabinets.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Where spatial constraints demand patch panels of different classification are located in the same cabinet, agencies MUST:

provide a physical barrier within the cabinet to separate patch panels;
ensure that only personnel cleared to the highest classification of the circuits in the panel have access to the cabinet; and
obtain approval from the relevant Accreditation Authority prior to installation.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD record equipment layouts and other relevant information on rack diagrams.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All earthing points MUST be equipotentially bonded.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All conductive elements of a cabinet, rack or case housing any equipment MUST be earth bonded.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Cabinet rails MUST be installed to:

provide adequate room for patch cables and wire managers;
provide adequate space for cable management at front, sides, and rear; and
arrange switches and patch panels to minimize patching between cabinets & racks.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD implement the principles and specific cable labelling requirements described above.

controls.title: Patch Panels, Patch Cables and Racks

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD follow best practice described above for the installation of power cables.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies designing and installing systems with RF transmitters within or co-located with their facility MUST:

contact GCSB for guidance on conducting an emanation security threat assessment; and
install cabling and equipment in accordance with this manual plus any specific installation criteria derived from the emanation security threat assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies designing and installing systems with RF transmitters that co-locate with systems of a classification CONFIDENTIAL and above MUST:

contact GCSB for guidance on conducting an emanation security threat assessment; and
install cabling and equipment in accordance with this manual plus any specific installation criteria derived from the emanation security threat assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies deploying systems overseas in temporary, mobile or fixed locations MUST:

contact GCSB for assistance with conducting an emanation security threat assessment; and
install cabling and equipment in accordance with this manual plus any specific installation criteria derived from the emanation security threat assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies deploying systems overseas SHOULD:

contact GCSB for assistance with conducting an emanation security threat advice; and
install cabling and equipment in accordance with this document plus any specific installation criteria derived from the emanation security threat assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD conduct an emanation security threat assessment as early as possible in project lifecycles.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that IT equipment within secure areas meet industry and government standards relating to electromagnetic interference/electromagnetic compatibility.

controls.title: Network Design, Architecture and IP Address Management

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST conduct and document a risk assessment before creating an architecture, and designing an agency network.

controls.title: Network Design, Architecture and IP Address Management

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The principles of separation and segregation as well as the system classification MUST be incorporated into the risk assessment.

controls.title: Network Design, Architecture and IP Address Management

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Security architectures MUST apply the principles of separation and segregation.

controls.title: Network Design, Architecture and IP Address Management

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The classification and other restrictions on the security and control of information MUST be clearly identified for each part of the Agency network.

controls.title: Network Design, Architecture and IP Address Management

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Systems of different classifications MUST be visually distinct.

controls.title: Network Design, Architecture and IP Address Management

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: An information repository, containing essential network information, change records and business requirements SHOULD be established and maintained.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies wishing to permit the use of Bluetooth MUST develop a policy that details the circumstances under which Bluetooth usage is permitted, and situations where it is not to be used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The policy position MUST include information about Bluetooth security controls that are to be used, and methods for verifying that the controls are in place and are effective.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that Bluetooth pairing is only established between authorised devices. (Unless a gateway is being used, paired devices are considered to operate at the same security classification level).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that Bluetooth discovery of devices is disabled unless a new pairing connection is being established.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that Bluetooth device pairing only occurs at a location where only authorised persons have access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that Bluetooth pairings are removed when they are no longer required.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies using Bluetooth MUST use the most secure configuration supported by the paired devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD identify the:

Bluetooth type (BR/EDR or LE), 
version, and
security capabilities;

for devices used to form Bluetooth connections, and ensure they are used to inform risk decisions on the use of Bluetooth.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that new Bluetooth connections between devices are authenticated using explicit user actions, such as entry of a numeric code, confirmation of a matching PIN, or other affirming action, such as challenge-response process.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies using Bluetooth between devices to transfer UNCLASSIFIED or IN-CONFIDENCE information SHOULD ensure that connections meet NZISM standards for authentication and use Approved Cryptographic Algorithms for encryption and message integrity.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies using Bluetooth between devices to transfer RESTRICTED or SENSITIVE information MUST ensure that connections meet NZISM standards for authentication and use Approved Cryptographic Algorithms for encryption and message integrity.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If Bluetooth specifications do not support these approved encryption methods, organisations MUST do a risk assessment and use the exception or waiver process to accept this risk.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST complete a technical evaluation of the secure area, consult the relevant technical authority and seek approval from the Accreditation Authority before permitting the use of Bluetooth devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies using Bluetooth devices MUST NOT allow:

line of sight and reflected communications travelling into an unsecure area.
multiple Bluetooth devices at different classifications in the same area.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT allow Bluetooth devices into secure areas unless authorised by the Accreditation Authority.

controls.title: Radio frequency and infrared devices in secure areas

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST prevent RF devices from being brought into secure areas unless authorised by the Accreditation Authority.

controls.title: Radio frequency and infrared devices in secure areas

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD prevent RF devices from being brought into secure areas unless authorised by the Accreditation Authority.

controls.title: Radio frequency and infrared devices in secure areas

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD limit the effective range of communications outside the agency’s area of control by:

minimising the output power level of wireless devices; 
RF shielding; and
Physical layout and separation.

controls.title: Radio frequency and infrared devices in secure areas

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD deploy measures to detect and respond to active RF devices within secure areas.

controls.title: Radio frequency and infrared devices in secure areas

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Wireless RF or IR pointing devices MUST NOT be used in secure areas unless approved by the Accreditation Authority and appropriate RF or IR mitigations are implemented.

controls.title: Radio frequency and infrared devices in secure areas

parts.name: statement

controls.title: Radio frequency and infrared devices in secure areas

parts.name: statement

controls.title: Radio frequency and infrared devices in secure areas

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies using IR devices SHOULD ensure that the IR receiver/port is positioned to prevent line of sight from the secure area boundary.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop a policy governing the use of telephones and telephone systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST advise personnel of the maximum permitted classification for conversations using both internal and external telephone connections.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD advise personnel of the audio security risk posed by using telephones in areas where classified conversations can occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies permitting different levels of conversation for different types of connections MUST use telephones that give a visual indication of the classification of the connection made.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies intending to use telephone systems for the transmission of classified information MUST ensure that:

the system has been accredited for the purpose; and
all classified traffic that passes over external systems is appropriately encrypted.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT use cordless telephones for classified conversations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD NOT use cordless telephones for classified or sensitive conversations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT use cordless telephones in conjunction with secure telephony devices.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that off-hook audio protection features are used on all telephones that are not accredited for the transmission of classified information in areas where such information could be discussed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST use push-to-talk mechanisms to meet the requirement for off-hook audio protection. PTT activation MUST be clearly labelled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that off-hook audio protection features are used on all telephones that are not accredited for the transmission of classified information in areas where such information could be discussed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST remove unused voice mailboxes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST expire and archive or delete voicemail messages after the retention period determined by the agency’s electronic records retention policy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD develop and implement a policy to manage the retention and disposal of such electronic records, including voicemail, email and other electronic records.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop a policy governing the use of mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST advise personnel of the maximum permitted classification for conversations using both internal and external mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD advise personnel of all known security risks posed by using mobile devices in areas where classified conversations can occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies intending to use mobile devices for the transmission of classified information MUST ensure that:

the network has been certified and accredited for the purpose; 
all classified traffic that passes over mobile devices is appropriately encrypted; and
users are aware of the area, surroundings, potential for overhearing and potential for oversight when using the device.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices MUST be prevented from entering secure areas.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD provide a storage area or lockers where mobile devices can be stored before personnel enter secure or protected areas.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop a policy governing the use of personal wearable devices, including fitness devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST advise personnel of the maximum permitted classification for conversations where any personal wearable or mobile device may be present.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD advise personnel of all known security risks posed by using personal wearable devices in secure areas or other areas where classified conversations can occur.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personal wearable devices MUST NOT be allowed to enter secure areas.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD provide a storage area or lockers where personal wearable devices can be stored before personnel enter secure or protected areas.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose:
Where personal wearable devices are exempted on medical grounds and used in secure areas agencies MUST ensure that:

the agency networks in secure areas have been certified and accredited for the purpose; and
users are aware of the area, surroundings, potential for overhearing and potential for oversight.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where the use of personal wearable devices is permitted on medical grounds and used within a corporate or agency environment, agencies MUST ensure any relevant legislation and regulation pertaining to the protection of personal information is followed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST conduct and document a risk assessment before implementing or adopting an RFID solution.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: This risk assessment MUST be the basis of a security architecture design.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop a strong security architecture to protect RFID databases and RFID systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST minimise the information stored on RFID tags and in the RFID subsystem.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD disable any rewrite functions on RFID devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD apply the access control requirements of the NZISM (Chapter 11 - Communications Systems and Devices) to RFID systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD develop, implement and maintain an RFID Usage Policy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD incorporate RFID into the agency’s security policies and information security documentation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST conduct visual and technical inspections of packaging and devices to determine if RFID devices have been attached and either permanently disable or remove such devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD conduct visual inspections of packaging and devices to determine if RFID devices have been attached and if these RFID devices pose a security concern.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD conduct visual inspections of packaging and devices to determine if RFID devices or labelling have been tampered with and whether this is a security concern.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider undertaking an RF engineering assessment where security concerns exist or where the RFID systems are to be used in areas with high levels of RF activity.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Shielding SHOULD be considered where eavesdropping or RF radiation is a concern, as determined by the risk assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider placement of tags and location of readers to avoid unnecessary electromagnetic radiation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST follow the requirements of the NZISM in the selection and implementation of cryptographic protocols and algorithms, and in key management, detailed in Chapter 17 - Cryptography.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD encrypt data before it is written to RFID tags.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD assign RFID identifiers using formats that limit information about tagged items or about the agency operating the RFID system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider the use of HMAC when tag authenticity is required.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST assign passwords for critical RFID functions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD follow the guidance for passwords management in the NZISM (Chapter 16 – Access control and passwords).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider temporary deactivation of RFID tags where the tag is likely to be inactive for extended periods.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop and implement incident identification and management processes in accordance with this manual (See Chapter 5 – Information Security Documentation, Chapter 6 – Information Security Monitoring, Chapter 7 – Information Security Incidents, Chapter 9 – Personnel Security and Chapter 16 – Access Control and passwords).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider secure disposal procedures and incorporate these into the RFID Usage Policy. Refer also to Chapter 13 – Media and IT equipment management, decommissioning and disposal.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop and implement user awareness and training programmes to support and enable safe use of RFID services (See Section 9.1 – Information Security Awareness and Training).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST conduct and document a risk assessment before implementing or adopting an RFID access card system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: This risk assessment MUST be the basis of a security architecture design.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop a strong security architecture to protect access to databases and systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD apply the NZISM access controls (Chapter 11) and cryptographic controls (Chapter 17) to access card systems.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD develop, implement and maintain an Access Card Usage Policy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD incorporate access cards into the agency’s security policies and information security documentation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD select systems that provide resistance to physical or electronic tampering.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD implement systems to minimise the risk of physical or electronic tampering.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider placement of tags and location of readers to avoid unnecessary electromagnetic radiation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider and select other physical controls in accordance with the PSR.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST follow the requirements of the NZISM in the selection and implementation of cryptographic protocols and algorithms, and in key management, detailed in Chapter 17 - Cryptography.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD encrypt data before it is written to cards.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider the use of cards systems incorporating Secure Access Modules (SAMs).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop and implement incident identification and management processes in accordance with this manual (See Chapter 5 – Information Security Documentation, Chapter 6 – Information Security Monitoring, Chapter 7 – Information Security Incidents, Chapter 9 – Personnel Security and Chapter 16 – Access control and passwords).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider secure disposal procedures and incorporate these into the Access Card Usage Policy. Refer also to Media and IT equipment management, decommissioning and disposal.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop a policy governing the use of MFDs, network printers and fax machines,

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies sending classified messages MUST ensure that the message is encrypted to an appropriate level when communicated over unsecured telecommunications infrastructure or the public switched telephone network.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST have separate MFDs or fax machines for sending classified messages and messages classified RESTRICTED and below.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
The sender of a fax message SHOULD make arrangements for the receiver to:

collect the fax message as soon as possible after it is received; and
notify the sender immediately if the fax message does not arrive when expected.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT enable a direct connection from a MFD to a telephone network unless the telephone network is accredited to at least the same classification as the computer network to which the device is connected.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD NOT enable a direct connection from a MFD to a telephone network unless the telephone network is accredited to at least the same classification as the computer network to which the device is connected.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Where MFDs connected to computer networks have the ability to communicate via a gateway to another network, agencies MUST ensure that:

each MFD applies user identification, authentication and audit functions for all classified information communicated by that device;
these mechanisms are of similar strength to those specified for workstations on that network; and
each gateway can identify and filter the classified information in accordance with the requirements for the export of data through a gateway.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT permit MFDs connected to computer networks to be used to scan or copy classified documents above the classification of the connected network.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that MFDs and fax machines are located in areas where their use can be observed.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any maintenance or servicing MUST be conducted under supervision or by cleared personnel.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any storage devices, drums or cartridges with memory chips removed during maintenance or servicing MUST be disposed of following the processes prescribed in Chapter 13 - Media and IT equipment Management, Decommissioning and Disposal.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Toner cartridges MUST have the memory chip removed before the cartridge is recycled or otherwise disposed of. The memory chip MUST be disposed of following the processes prescribed in Chapter 13 - Media and IT equipment Management, Decommissioning and Disposal.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any maintenance or servicing SHOULD be conducted under supervision or by cleared personnel.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any storage devices, drums or cartridges with memory chips removed during maintenance or servicing SHOULD be disposed of following the processes prescribed in Chapter 13 - Media and IT equipment Management, Decommissioning and Disposal.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The use of any USB capability MUST be conducted under supervision or by cleared personnel.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The use of any USB capability SHOULD be conducted under supervision or by cleared personnel.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any storage devices, drums, cartridge memory chips or other components that may contain data or copies of documents MUST be disposed of following the processes prescribed in Chapter 13 - Media and IT equipment Management, Decommissioning and Disposal.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any storage devices, drums, cartridge memory chips or other components that may contain data or copies of documents SHOULD be disposed of following the processes prescribed in Chapter 13 - Media and IT equipment Management, Decommissioning and Disposal.

controls.title: Multifunction Devices, Network Printers and Fax Machines

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Use of MFDs for printing, scanning, and copying purposes SHOULD be centrally logged.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: When choosing a product, agencies MUST document the justification for any decision to choose a product that is still in evaluation and accept any security risk introduced by the use of such a product.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD select products that have their desired security functionality within the scope of the product’s evaluation and are applicable to the agency’s intended environment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST check consumer guides for products, where available, to determine any product specific requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where product specific requirements exist in a consumer guide, agencies MUST comply with the requirements outlined in the consumer guide.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies selecting high assurance products and HACE MUST contact the GCSB and comply with any product specific requirements, before any purchase is made.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD:

obtain software from verifiable sources and verify its integrity using vendor supplied checksums; and
validate the software’s interaction with the operating systems and network within a test environment prior to use on operational systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies procuring high assurance products and HACE MUST contact the GCSB and comply with any product specific delivery procedures.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that products are delivered in a manner consistent with any delivery procedures defined in associated documentation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD ensure that products purchased without the delivery assurances provided through the use of formally evaluated procedures are delivered in a manner that provides confidence that they receive the product that they expect to receive in an unaltered state, including checking:

any labelling changes;
any damage; and
any signs of tampering.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD choose products from developers that have made a commitment to the ongoing maintenance of the assurance of their product.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that high assurance products and HACE are installed, configured, operated and administered in accordance with all product specific policy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD install, configure, operate and administer evaluated products in accordance with available documentation resulting from the product’s evaluation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies wishing to use a product in an unevaluated configuration MUST undertake a security risk assessment including:

the necessity of the unevaluated configuration;
testing of the unevaluated configuration; and
the environment in which the unevaluated product is to be used.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: High assurance products and HACE MUST NOT be used in unevaluated configurations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST classify IT equipment based on the highest classification of information the equipment and any associated media within the equipment, are approved for processing, storing or communicating.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST clearly label all IT equipment capable of storing or processing classified information, with the exception of HACE, with the appropriate protective marking.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST clearly label all IT equipment in data centres or computer rooms with an asset identification and the level of classification to which that equipment has been accredited.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT have any non-essential labels applied to external surfaces of high assurance products.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD seek GCSB authorisation before applying labels to external surfaces of HACE.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD monitor relevant sources for information about new vulnerabilities and security patches for software and IT equipment used by the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST apply all critical security patches as soon as possible and within two (2) days of the release of the patch or update.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST implement a patch management strategy, including an evaluation or testing process.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT patch high assurance products or HACE without the patch being approved by the GCSB.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD apply all critical security patches as soon as possible and preferably within two (2) days of the release of the patch or update.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD apply all non-critical security patches as soon as possible.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that security patches are applied through a vendor recommended patch or upgrade process.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that any firmware updates are performed in a manner that verifies the integrity and authenticity of the source and of the updating process or updating utility.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD assess the security risk of continued use of software or IT equipment when a cessation date for support is announced or when the product is no longer supported by the developer.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST seek GCSB approval before undertaking any repairs to high assurance products or HACE.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Maintenance and repairs of IT equipment containing media SHOULD be carried out on-site by an appropriately cleared technician.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: If an uncleared technician is used to undertake maintenance or repairs of IT equipment, agencies SHOULD sanitise and reclassify or declassify the equipment and associated media before maintenance or repair work is undertaken.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that the ratio of escorts to uncleared technicians allows for appropriate oversight of all activities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If an uncleared technician is used to undertake maintenance or repairs of IT equipment, the technician SHOULD be escorted by someone who is sufficiently familiar with the product to understand the work being performed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies having IT equipment maintained or repaired off-site MUST ensure that the physical transfer, processing and storage requirements are appropriate for the classification of the product and are maintained at all times.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Offsite repairs and maintenance SHOULD treat all equipment in accordance with the requirements for the highest classification of information processed, stored or communicated in the area that the equipment will be returned to.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD conduct or arrange to have technical inspections conducted on all equipment returned to the secure area after maintenance or repair.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST sanitise or destroy, then declassify, IT equipment containing any media before disposal.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: IT equipment and associated media that have processed or stored NZEO information, and cannot be sanitised, MUST be returned to New Zealand for sanitisation or destruction, declassification and disposal.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST have a documented process for the disposal of IT equipment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST contact the GCSB and comply with any requirements for the disposal of high assurance products.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST contact the GCSB and comply with any requirements for the disposal of HACE.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST contact GCSB and comply with any requirements for the disposal of TEMPEST rated IT equipment or if the equipment is non-functional.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST formally sanitise and then authorise the disposal of IT equipment, or waste, into the public domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST print at least three pages of random text with no blank areas on each colour printer cartridge with an integrated drum or separate copier drum.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD print at least three pages of random text with no blank areas on each colour printer cartridge with an integrated drum or separate copier drum.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies unable to sanitise printer cartridges with integrated copier drums or discrete copier drums, MUST destroy the cartridge or drum.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies unable to sanitise printer cartridges with integrated copier drums or discrete copier drums, SHOULD destroy the cartridge or drum.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST visually inspect video screens by turning up the brightness to the maximum level to determine if any classified information has been burnt into or persists on the screen, before redeployment or disposal.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST attempt to sanitise video screens with minor burn-in or image persistence by displaying a solid white image on the screen for an extended period of time. If burn-in cannot be corrected the screen MUST be processed through an approved destruction facility.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Because of the risks that data can be recovered from monitors, it is essential that any redeployment or disposal of monitors MUST follow the guidance in the NZISM.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD incorporate the consideration of supply chain risks into an organisation-wide risk assessment and management process.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD monitor supply chain risks on an ongoing basis and adjust mitigations and controls appropriately.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD follow the Government Rules of Procurement.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD require tenderers and contractors to provide information:

identifying any restrictions on the disclosure, transfer or use of technology arising out of export controls or security arrangements; and
demonstrating that their supply chains comply with the security of supply requirements set out in the contract documents.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD request information from contractors and subcontractors to assess their ability to protect information.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST include contractual obligations on all contractors and subcontractors to safeguard information throughout the tendering and contracting procedure.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD include contractual obligations to safeguard information throughout the tendering and contracting procedure.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD reject contractors and subcontractors where they do not possess the necessary reliability to exclude risks to national security; or have breached obligations relating to security of information during a previous contract in circumstances amounting to grave misconduct.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that changes in their supply chain during the performance of the contract will not adversely affect the continuity of supply requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST require suppliers and contractors to provide the provenance of any products or equipment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD require suppliers and contractors to provide the provenance of any products or equipment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD validate the security of the equipment against security performance, capability and functionality requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where deficiencies in security performance, capability and functionality are identified, agencies SHOULD implement additional risk mitigation measures.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST consult with the GCSB on pre-installation, security verification and related tests before the equipment is used in an operational system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD inspect equipment on receipt for any obvious signs of tampering, relabelling or damage.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD inspect equipment on receipt and test the operation before installation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD conduct installation verification and related tests before the equipment is used in an operational system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where any software, firmware or other forms of programme code are required for the initialisation, operation, servicing or maintenance of the equipment, malware checks SHOULD be conducted before the equipment is installed in an operational system.

parts.name: statement

groups.title: 13. Media and IT Equipment Management, Decommissioning and Disposal

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When the Information System reaches the end of its service life in an organisation, policy and procedures SHOULD be in place to ensure secure decommissioning and transfer or disposal, in order to satisfy corporate, legal and statutory requirements.

parts.name: statement
statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider the need for a Privacy Impact Assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD identify relevant service and legal agreements and arrange for their termination.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD identify data retention policies, regulation and legislation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure adequate system documentation is archived.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD archive essential software, system logic, system documentation and other system data to allow information to be recovered from archive.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Agency’s Accreditation Authority SHOULD confirm IA compliance on decommissioning and disposal.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Agency’s Accreditation Authority SHOULD confirm secure equipment and media disposal.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The Agency’s Accreditation Authority SHOULD confirm asset register updates.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Once all security relevant activities associated with decommissioning and disposal have been completed and verified, a Security Decommissioning Compliance Certificate SHOULD be issued by the Agency’s Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD undertake a post-decommissioning review.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST document procedures for the reclassification and declassification of media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST classify media to the highest classification of data stored on the media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST classify any media connected to a system of a higher classification at the higher system classification until confirmed not to be the case.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies intending to classify media below the classification of the system to which it is connected to MUST ensure that:

the media is read-only;
the media is inserted into a read-only device; or
the system has a mechanism through which read-only access can be assured such as approved data diodes, write-blockers or similar infrastructure.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies wishing to reclassify media to a lower classification MUST ensure that:

a formal decision is made to reclassify, or redeploy the media; and
the reclassification of all information on the media has been approved by the originator, or the media has been appropriately sanitised or destroyed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST reclassify media if:

information copied onto the media is of a higher classification; or
information contained on the media is subjected to a classification upgrade.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST label media with a marking that indicates the maximum classification and any endorsements applicable to the information stored.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that the classification of all media is easily visually identifiable.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When using non-textual (colour, symbol) protective markings for operational security reasons, agencies MUST document the labelling scheme and train personnel appropriately.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD label media with a marking that indicates the maximum classification and any endorsements applicable to the information stored.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST label non-volatile media that has been sanitised and reclassified for redeployment with a notice similar to:

Warning: media has been sanitised and reclassified from [classification] to [classification]. Further lowering of classification only via destruction.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST register all media with a unique identifier in an appropriate register.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD register all media with a unique identifier in an appropriate register.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT use media containing classified information with a system that has a classification lower than the classification of the media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that storage facilities for media containing classified information meets the minimum physical security storage requirements as specified in the Protective Security Requirements (PSR).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST disable any automatic execution features within operating systems for connectable devices and media.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: When writable media is connected to a writable communications port or device, agencies SHOULD implement controls to prevent the unintended writing of data to the media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST disable IEEE 1394 interfaces.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD disable IEEE 1394 interfaces.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that processes for transferring media containing classified information meets the minimum physical transfer requirements as specified in the PSR.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD encrypt data stored on media with at least an Approved Cryptographic Algorithm (See Section 17.2 – Approved Cryptographic Algorithms) if it is to be transferred to another area or location.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Data transfers between systems of different classification SHOULD be logged in an auditable log or register.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies transferring data manually between two systems of different security domains or classifications SHOULD NOT use rewriteable media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT permit any media that uses external interface connections within a TOP SECRET area without prior written approval from the Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST document conditions and procedures for the sanitisation of media and IT Equipment.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST sanitise volatile media by:

overwriting all locations of the media with an arbitrary pattern;
followed by a read back for verification; and
removing power from the media for at least 10 minutes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Following sanitisation, volatile media MUST be treated as indicated in the table below.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST sanitise non-volatile magnetic media by:

if pre-2001 or under 15GB: overwriting the media at least three times in its entirety with an arbitrary pattern followed by a read back for verification; or
if post-2001 or over 15GB: overwriting the media at least once in its entirety with an arbitrary pattern followed by a read back for verification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST boot from separate media to the media being sanitised when undertaking sanitisation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD reset the host protected area and drive configuration overlay table of non-volatile magnetic hard disks prior to overwriting the media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD attempt to overwrite the growth defects table (g-list) on non-volatile magnetic hard disks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use the ATA security erase command for sanitising non-volatile magnetic hard disks instead of using block overwriting software.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Following sanitisation, non-volatile magnetic media MUST be treated as indicated in the table below.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST sanitise non-volatile EPROM media by erasing as per the manufacturer’s specification, increasing the specified ultraviolet erasure time by a factor of three, then overwriting the media at least once in its entirety with a pseudo random pattern, followed by a read back for verification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST sanitise non-volatile EEPROM media by overwriting the media at least once in its entirety with a pseudo random pattern, followed by a read back for verification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Following sanitisation, non-volatile EPROM and EEPROM media MUST be treated as indicated in the table below.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST sanitise non-volatile flash memory media by overwriting the media at least twice in its entirety with a pseudo random pattern, followed by a read back for verification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Following sanitisation, non-volatile flash memory media MUST be treated as indicated in the table below.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where manufacturer sanitation procedures cannot be determined, items MUST be destroyed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Solid state drives MUST be destroyed before disposal.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Solid state drives MUST be sanitised using ATA Secure Erase sanitation software before redeployment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Solid state drives MUST NOT be redeployed in a lower classification environment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Hybrid drives MUST be treated as solid state drives for sanitisation purposes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD sanitise all media and IT Equipment prior to reuse at the same or higher classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD verify the sanitisation of media and IT Equipment using a different product from the one used to perform the initial sanitisation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST document procedures for the destruction of media and IT Equipment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Media and IT equipment destruction MUST be performed using approved destruction equipment, facilities and methods.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where agencies do not own their own destruction equipment, agencies MUST use an approved destruction facility for media and IT equipment destruction.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where a facility is NOT an approved facility, agencies MUST ensure any incineration equipment is rated for the destruction of electronic waste (WEEE) and the operator is properly authorised or licensed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where a facility is NOT an approved facility, agencies MUST ensure processes are in place for the safe handling of electronic waste (WEEE), including any residual material from the destruction process.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST, at minimum, store and handle the resulting waste for all methods, in accordance with the classification given in the table below.
