parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST undertake a threat and risk assessment on the use of inverse split tunnelling prior to enabling the functionality in remote access VPN systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
When providing inverse split-tunnelled access to internet based services (“directly accessed services”), the following aspects SHOULD be considered as part of the threat and risk assessment:

How do directly accessed services authenticate agency device identities prior to granting access to the service?
How do agency devices securely resolve internet addresses for directly accessed services?
How are the communications between the devices and directly accessed services secured?
How does an agency monitor and account for access made to directly accessed services?
How does an agency protect devices from compromise if they are able to directly access internet based resources, or be directly accessed from the internet?
How do directly accessed services authenticate the user of the agency device prior to granting access to the service (this is separate to authenticating the agency device itself)?
How does an agency enforce the use of multi-factor authentication with directly accessed services?
How does an agency authorise access to directly accessed services, and does this include from devices that are not authorised to connect to agency remote access services (authorisation and authentication are separate activities)?
How is access to directly accessed cloud services removed when staff no longer require access or leave the agency?

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When agencies have cascaded connections between networks involving multiple gateways they MUST ensure that the assurance levels specified for network devices between the overall lowest and highest networks are met by the gateway between the highest network and the next highest network within the cascaded connection.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST ensure that:

all agency networks are protected from networks in other security domains by one or more gateways;
all gateways contain mechanisms to filter or limit data flow at the network and content level to only the information necessary for business purposes; and
all gateway components, discrete and virtual, are physically located within an appropriately secured server room.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: For gateways between networks in different security domains, any shared components MUST be managed by the system owners of the highest security domain or by a mutually agreed party.

parts.name: statement
statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST use demilitarised zones to house systems and information directly accessed externally.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use demilitarised zones to house systems and information directly accessed externally.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST perform a risk assessment on gateways and their configuration prior to their implementation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All domain and system owners connected through a gateway MUST understand and accept the residual security risk of the gateway and from any connected domains including those via a cascaded connection.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD annually review the security architecture of the gateway and risks of all connected domains including those via a cascaded connection.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Once connectivity is established, domain owners MUST be considered information stakeholders for all connected domains.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Once connectivity is established, domain owners SHOULD be considered information stakeholders for all connected domains.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All system users MUST be trained on the secure use and security risks of the gateways before being granted access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All system users SHOULD be trained in the secure use and security risks of the gateways before being granted access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST limit access to gateway administration functions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that system administrators are formally trained to manage gateways by qualified trainers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that all system administrators of gateways that process NZEO information meet the nationality requirements for these endorsements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST separate roles for the administration of gateways (e.g.  separate network and security policy configuration roles).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD separate roles for the administration of gateways (e.g. separate network and security policy configuration roles).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST authenticate system users to all classified networks accessed through gateways.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that only authenticated and authorised system users can use the gateway.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use multi-factor authentication for access to networks and gateways.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD authenticate any IT equipment that connects to networks accessed through gateways.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST undertake a risk assessment and update the SRMP before changes are implemented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST document any changes to gateways in accordance with the agency’s Change Management Policy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD undertake a risk assessment and update the SRMP before changes are implemented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that testing of security measures is performed at random intervals no more than six months apart.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: For the purposes of this Manual, the CDS MUST be classified at the highest classification of connected domains.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies connecting a TOP SECRET, SECRET OR CONFIDENTIAL network to any other network MUST implement a CDS.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST NOT implement a gateway permitting data to flow directly from:

a TOP SECRET network to any network below SECRET;
a SECRET network to an UNCLASSIFIED network; or
a CONFIDENTIAL network to an UNCLASSIFIED network.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When designing and deploying a CDS, agencies MUST consult with the GCSB and comply with all directions provided.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies connecting a typical gateway and a CDS to a common network MUST consult the GCSB on the impact to the security of the CDS and comply with all directions provided.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that all bi-directional gateways between TOP SECRET and SECRET networks, SECRET and less classified networks, and CONFIDENTIAL and less classified networks, have separate upward and downward paths which use a diode and physically separate infrastructure for each path.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Trusted sources MUST be:

a strictly limited list derived from business requirements and the result of a security risk assessment;
where necessary an appropriate security clearance is held; and
approved by the Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Trusted sources MUST authorise all data to be exported from a security domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All data exported from a security domain MUST be logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All gateways MUST contain a firewall in both physical and virtual environments.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST check the evaluation has examined the security enforcing functions by reviewing the target of evaluation/security target and other testing documentation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
The requirement to implement a firewall as part of gateway architecture MUST be met separately and independently by both parties (gateways) in both physical and virtual environments.

Shared equipment DOES NOT satisfy the requirements of this control.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST use a firewall of at least an EAL4 assurance level between an NZEO network and a foreign network in addition to the minimum assurance levels for firewalls between networks of different classifications or security domains.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In all other circumstances the table at 19.3.8.C.03 MUST apply.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use a firewall of at least an EAL2 assurance level or a Protection Profile between an NZEO network and another New Zealand controlled network within a single security domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST use a diode of at least an EAL4 assurance level between an NZEO network and a foreign network in addition to the minimum assurance levels for diodes between networks of different classifications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In all other circumstances the table at 19.4.4.C.01 MUST apply.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use a diode of at least an EAL2 assurance level or a Protection Profile between an NZEO network and another New Zealand controlled network within a single security domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies deploying a diode to control data flow within one-way gateways SHOULD monitor the volume of the data being transferred.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt VoIP or UC technologies or services MUST conduct a comprehensive risk assessment before implementation or adoption.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt VoIP or UC technologies or services MUST consider the risks to the availability of systems and information in their design of VoIP and UC systems architecture, fault tolerance, fail over and supporting controls and governance processes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure risks for any VoIP or UC service adopted are understood and formally accepted by the agency’s Accreditation Authority as part of the Certification and Accreditation process (See Chapter 4 - System Certification and Accreditation).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt VoIP or UC technologies or services MUST determine where the responsibility (agency or VoIP and UC service provider) for implementing, managing and maintaining controls lies in accordance with agreed trust boundaries.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any contracts for the provision of VoIP or UC services MUST include service level, availability, recoverability and restoration provisions as formally determined by business requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure contracts with VoIP or UC service providers include provisions to manage risks associated with the merger, acquisition, liquidation or bankruptcy of the service provider and any subsequent termination of VoIP or UC services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies procuring or using VoIP or UC services to be used by multiple agencies MUST ensure all interested parties formally agree to the risks, controls and any residual risks of such VoIP and UC services.  The lead agency normally has this responsibility (see Chapter 2 - Information Security services within Government and Chapter 4 - System Certification and Accreditation).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider the use of assessment tools, such as penetration testing, when undertaking the risk assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST follow the gateway requirements described in Chapter 19 - Gateway Security.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt VoIP or UC technologies or services MUST determine trust boundaries before implementation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Updates to the SBC and related devices MUST be verified by the administrator to ensure they are obtained from a trusted source and are unaltered.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST include defence mechanisms for the Common VoIP and UC Security Risks and Threats described in 19.5.10.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agency networks MUST ensure the SBC includes a topology hiding capability.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agency networks MUST consider the use of call diversity and call failover configurations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In a virtualised environment, agencies MUST ensure any data contained in a protected resource is deleted or not available when the virtual resource is reallocated.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD conduct a traffic analysis to ensure the agency’s network and architecture is capable of supporting all VoIP, media and UC traffic.  The traffic analysis SHOULD also determine any high availability requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD design a security and gateway architecture that segregates UC and normal data traffic.  Firewall requirements (Section 19.3 - Firewalls) continue to apply to data traffic.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In a virtualised environment, agencies SHOULD create separate virtual LANs for data traffic and UC traffic.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In a non-virtualised environment, agencies SHOULD create separate LANs for data traffic and UC traffic.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agency networks SHOULD use encryption internally on VoIP and unified communications traffic.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agency networks SHOULD ensure intrusion prevention systems and firewalls are VoIP-aware.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any shared facilities MUST be clearly identifiable both physically and logically.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST provide a protected communication channel for administrators, and authorised systems personnel.  Such communication MUST be logged.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure administrative access to the SBC is available only through a trusted LAN and secure communication path.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access control and password requirements SHOULD apply to VoIP and UC networks in all cases where individual access is granted.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: In special cases where individual UserIDs and Passwords are impractical, a risk assessment SHOULD be completed and compensating controls applied.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Event logs covering all VoIP and UC services SHOULD be maintained in accordance with the requirements of the NZISM. See sections 16.6 - Event Logging and Auditing and 13.1.12 - Archiving.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST include incident handling and management services in contracts with service providers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop and implement incident identification and management processes in accordance with this manual (See Chapter 6 – Information Security Monitoring, Chapter 7 – Information Security Incidents, Chapter 9 – Personnel Security and Chapter 16 – Access control and passwords).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD implement fraud detection monitoring to identify suspicious activity and provide alerting so that remedial action can be taken.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD regularly review call detail records for patterns of service theft.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider the use of allow and deny listing to manage fraudulent calls to known fraudulent call destinations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider the use of call rate limiting as a fraud mitigation measure.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider the use of call redirection to manage blocked calls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop and implement user awareness and training programmes to support and enable safe use of VoIP and UC services (See Section 9.1 – Information Security Awareness and Training).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The use of cloud services and infrastructures for systems and data classified CONFIDENTIAL, SECRET or TOP SECRET MUST be approved by the GCSB.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt cloud technologies or services MUST ensure cloud service providers apply the controls specified in this manual to any systems hosting, processing or storing agency data and systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT use public, hybrid (incorporating a public element), or other external cloud services for systems and data classified CONFIDENTIAL, SECRET or TOP SECRET.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT use public or hybrid (incorporating a public element) cloud services to host, process, store or transmit NZEO endorsed information.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt cloud technologies or services SHOULD obtain formal assurance cloud service providers will apply the controls specified in this manual to any cloud service hosting, processing or storing agency data and systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt cloud technologies or services MUST conduct a risk assessment before implementation or adoption.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt cloud technologies or services MUST determine trust boundaries before implementation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt cloud technologies or services MUST determine where the responsibility (agency or cloud service provider) for implementing, managing and maintaining controls lies in accordance with agreed trust boundaries.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure cloud risks for any cloud service adopted are understood and formally accepted by the Agency Head or Chief Executive (or their formal delegate) and the agency’s Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST consult with the GCDO to ensure the strategic and other cloud risks are comprehensively assessed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies procuring or using cloud services to be used by multiple agencies MUST ensure all interested parties formally agree the risks, controls and any residual risks of such cloud services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies using cloud services MUST ensure they have conducted a documented risk assessment, accepted any residual risks, and followed the endorsement procedure required by the GCDO.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies using cloud services hosted offshore MUST ensure jurisdictional, sovereignty and privacy risks are fully considered and formally accepted by the Agency Head or Chief Executive and the agency’s Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies using cloud services hosted offshore MUST ensure that the agency retains ownership of its information in any contract with the cloud service provider.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies using cloud services hosted offshore and connected to All-of-Government systems MUST ensure they have conducted a risk assessment, accepted any residual risks, and followed the endorsement procedure required by the GCDO.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT use cloud services hosted offshore for information or systems classified CONFIDENTIAL, SECRET or TOP SECRET.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT use cloud services hosted offshore for information with an NZEO endorsement.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt cloud technologies or services MUST consider the risks to the availability of systems and information in their design of cloud systems architectures and supporting controls and governance processes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any contracts for the provision of cloud services MUST include service level, availability, recoverability and restoration provisions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure contracts with cloud service providers include provisions to manage risks associated with the merger, acquisition, liquidation or bankruptcy of the service provider and any subsequent termination of cloud services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt cloud technologies or services SHOULD ensure cloud service providers apply the physical, virtual and access controls specified in this manual for agency systems and data protection.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt cloud technologies or services SHOULD apply separation and access controls to protect data and systems where support is provided by offshore technical staff.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt cloud technologies or services SHOULD apply controls to detect and prevent unauthorised data transfers and multiple or large scale data transfers to offshore locations and entities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies intending to adopt cloud technologies or services SHOULD consider the use of encryption for data in transit and at rest.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST include incident handling and management services in contracts with cloud service providers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop and implement incident identification and management processes in accordance with this manual (See Chapter 6 – Information Security Monitoring, Chapter 7 – Information Security Incidents, Chapter 9 – Personnel Security and Chapter 16 – Access Control).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop and implement a backup, recovery and archiving plan and supporting procedures (See Section 6.4 – Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST include a data purge or secure delete process in any cloud service contracts.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any data purge or secure delete process in any cloud service contracts MUST be independently verifiable.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop and implement user awareness and training programmes to support and enable safe use of cloud services (See Section 9.1 – Information Security Awareness and Training).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Virtualisation technology MUST NOT be used for functional segregation between servers of different classifications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Virtualisation technology MUST NOT be used for functional segregation between servers in different security domains at the same classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD ensure that functional segregation between servers is achieved by:

physically, using single dedicated machines for each function; or
using virtualisation technology to create separate virtual machines for each function within the same security domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Virtualisation technology SHOULD NOT be used for functional segregation between servers in different security domains at the same classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST undertake a virtualisation specific risk assessment in order to identify risks, related risk treatments and controls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD undertake a virtualisation specific risk assessment in order to identify risks and related risk treatments.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST architect virtualised systems and environments to enforce the principles of separation and segregation of key elements of the system using trust zones or security domains.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT permit the sharing of files or other operating system components between host and guest operating systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD architect virtualised systems and environments to enforce the principles of separation and segregation of key elements of the system using trust zones.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD design virtualised systems and environments to enable functional segregation within a security domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD harden the host operating systems following an agency or other approved hardening guide.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD separate production from test or development virtual environments.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD NOT permit the sharing of files or other operating system components between host and guest operating systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure a VM migration policy and related SOPs are implemented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST implement controls to prohibit unauthorised VM migrations within a virtual environment or between physical environments.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST implement controls to safely decommission VMs when no longer required, including elimination of images, snapshots, storage, backup, archives and any other residual data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure a VM migration policy and related SOPs are implemented.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD implement controls to prohibit unauthorised VM migrations within a virtual environment or between physical environments.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD implement controls to safely decommission VMs when no longer required.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST maintain strong physical security and physical access controls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST maintain strong authentication and access controls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD maintain strong data validation checks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The principles of separation and segregation MUST be applied to the design and architecture of VLANs.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT use VLANs between classified networks and any other network of a lower classification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT use VLANs between any classified network and any unclassified network.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: VLAN trunking MUST NOT be used on switches managing VLANs of differing security domains.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Administrative access MUST be permitted only from the most trusted network.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unused ports on the switches MUST be disabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Unused ports on the switches SHOULD be disabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST establish a policy and train staff in the processes for data transfers between systems and the authorisations required before transfers can take place.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that system users transferring data to and from a system are held accountable for the data they transfer.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that data transfers are performed in accordance with processes and procedures approved by the Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that data transfers are performed in accordance with processes and procedures approved by the Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that all data transferred to a system of a lesser classification or a less secure system, is approved by a trusted source.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Trusted sources MUST be:

a strictly limited list derived from business requirements and the result of a security risk assessment;
where necessary an appropriate security clearance is held; and
approved by the Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies importing data to a system MUST ensure that the data is scanned for malicious and active content.

parts.name: statement
statement
statement
statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST:

ensure that keyword searches are performed on all textual data;
ensure that any identified data is quarantined until reviewed and approved for release by a trusted source other than the originator; and
develop procedures to prevent NZEO information in both textual and non-textual formats from being exported.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Users transferring data to and from a system MUST be held accountable for the data they transfer.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: All data transferred to a system of a lesser sensitivity or classification MUST be approved by a trusted source.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Trusted sources MUST be:

a strictly limited list derived from business requirements and the result of a security risk assessment;
where necessary an appropriate security clearance is held; and
approved by the Accreditation Authority.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When agencies import data to a system through gateways, the data MUST be filtered by a product specifically designed for that purpose, including filtering malicious and active content.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When agencies import data to a system through gateways, full or partial audits of the event logs MUST be performed at least monthly.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD convert data being imported at gateways into an alternative format before entering the network.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD restrict the export of data to a system of a lesser classification by filtering data using at least protective marking checks.

parts.name: statement
statement
parts.prose: When agencies export data, other than highly formatted textual data, through gateways, agencies MUST implement data filtering performed by a product specifically designed for that purpose.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When agencies do not perform audits of the complete data transfer logs at least monthly they MUST perform randomly timed audits of random subsets of the data transfer logs on a weekly basis.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where the classification cannot be determined automatically, a human trusted source SHOULD assess the classification of the data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When the export of other data occurs through gateways agencies SHOULD perform audits of the complete data transfer logs at least monthly.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: To prevent the export of NZEO data to foreign systems, agencies MUST implement NZEO data filtering performed by a product specifically designed or configured for that purpose.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST undertake checks of protective markings and keywords before permitting data export.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A trusted source MUST sign the data to be exported if the data is to be communicated over a network to which untrusted personnel or systems have access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that the gateway verifies authority to release prior to the release of the data to be exported.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use a product evaluated to at least an EAL4 assurance level for the purpose of data signing and signature confirmation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST strictly define and limit the types of files that can be transferred based on business requirements and the results of a security risk assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD strictly define and limit the types of files that can be transferred based on business requirements and the results of a security risk assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST block all executables and active content from entering a security domain.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD block all executables and active content from being communicated though gateways.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST block, quarantine or drop any data identified by a data filter as suspicious until reviewed and approved for transfer by a trusted source other than the originator.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST perform validation on all data passing through a content filter, blocking content which fails the validation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD perform validation on all data passing through a content filter, blocking content which fails the validation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD perform content conversion, file conversion or both for all ingress or egress data transiting a security domain boundary.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD perform content and file sanitisation on suitable file types if content conversion or file conversion is not appropriate for data transiting a security domain boundary.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD perform antivirus scans on all content using up-to-date engines and signatures, using multiple different scanning engines.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD extract the contents from archive and container files and subject the extracted files to content filter tests.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD perform controlled inspection of archive and container files to ensure that content filter performance and availability is not adversely affected.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD block files that cannot be inspected and generate an alert or notification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST create and enforce an allow list of permitted content types based on business requirements and the results of a security risk assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD create and enforce an allow list of permitted content types based on business requirements and the results of a security risk assessment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If data is signed, agencies MUST ensure that the signature is validated before the data is exported.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD verify the integrity of content where applicable, and block the content if verification fails.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD decrypt and inspect all encrypted content, traffic and data to allow content filtering.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST use protective marking checks to restrict the export of data from each security domain, including through a gateway.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When importing data to each security domain, including through a gateway, agencies MUST audit the complete data transfer logs at least monthly.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD create an exception handling process to deal with blocked or quarantined file types that may have a valid requirement to be transferred.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST ensure that all classified information stored within a database is associated with an appropriate protective marking if the information:

could be exported to a different system; or
contains differing classifications or different handling requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that protective markings are applied with a level of granularity sufficient to clearly define the handling requirements for any classified information retrieved or exported from a database.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD ensure that all classified information stored within a database is associated with an appropriate protective marking if the information:

could be exported to a different system; or
contains differing classifications or different handling requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that protective markings are applied with a level of granularity sufficient to clearly define the handling requirements for any classified information retrieved or exported from a database.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST protect database files from access that bypasses the database’s normal access controls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD protect database files from access that bypass normal access controls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST enable logging and auditing of system users’ actions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that databases provide functionality to allow for auditing of system users’ actions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If results from database queries cannot be appropriately filtered, agencies MUST ensure that all query results are appropriately sanitised to meet the minimum security clearances of system users.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that system users who do not have sufficient security clearances to view database contents cannot see or interrogate associated metadata in a list of results from a search engine query.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop a policy governing the use of mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT allow mobile devices to process or store TOP SECRET information unless explicitly approved by GCSB to do so.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD implement a Mobile Device Management (MDM) solution.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST advise personnel of the maximum permitted classifications for data and voice communications when using mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD NOT use Paging Services, SMS or MMS for sensitive or classified communications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST apply the full set of BYOD controls for devices NOT directly owned and controlled by the agency.  These controls are detailed in Section 21.4 – BYOD.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies unable to lower the storage and physical transfer requirements of a mobile device to an unclassified level through the use of encryption MUST physically store or transfer the device as a classified asset in accordance with the relevant handling instructions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Users MUST NOT store passwords, passphrases, PINs or other access codes for encryption on or with the mobile device on which data will be encrypted when the device is issued for normal operations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies unable to lower the storage and physical transfer requirements of a mobile device to an unclassified level through the use of encryption SHOULD physically store or transfer the device as a classified asset in accordance with the relevant handling instructions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD encrypt classified information on all mobile devices using an Approved Cryptographic Algorithm.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Pool or shared devices SHOULD be reissued with unique passwords, passphrases, PINs or other access codes for each separate issue or deployment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST use encryption on mobile devices communicating over public infrastructure, the Internet or non-agency controlled networks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use encryption for Official Information or any classified information on mobile devices communicating over public infrastructure, the Internet or non-agency controlled networks.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD apply privacy filters to the screens of mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT enable Bluetooth functionality on mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD NOT enable Bluetooth functionality on mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agency personnel MUST NOT disable security functions or security configurations on a mobile device once provisioned.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD control the configuration of mobile devices in the same manner as devices in the agency’s office environment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD prevent personnel from installing unauthorised applications on a mobile device once provisioned.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that mobile devices have security updates applied on a regular basis and are tested to ensure that the mobile devices are still secure.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD conduct policy checks as mobile devices connect to agency systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST disable split tunnelling when using a VPN connection from a mobile device to connect to an agency network.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD NOT allow mobile devices to connect to the Internet except when temporarily connecting to facilitate the establishment of a VPN connection to an agency network.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST develop an emergency destruction plan for mobile devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: If a cryptographic zeroise or sanitise function is provided for cryptographic keys on a mobile device it MUST be used as part of the emergency destruction procedures.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure personnel are trained in emergency destruction procedures and are familiar with the emergency destruction plan.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD use soft labelling for mobile devices when appropriate to reduce their attractiveness value.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD develop a policy to manage the non-business or personal use of an agency owned device.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mobile devices SHOULD NOT be used other than by personnel specifically authorised by the agency.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT allow personnel to access or communicate classified information on mobile devices outside of secure areas unless there is a reduced chance of being overheard and having the screen of the device observed.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies allowing personnel to access or communicate classified information outside of the office SHOULD NOT allow personnel to do so in public locations (e.g. public transport, transit lounges, hotel lobbies and coffee shops).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies MUST ensure mobile devices are carried in a secured state when not being actively used, by:

power off; or
power on but pass code enabled.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When in use mobile devices MUST be kept under continual direct supervision.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When travelling with mobile devices and media, personnel MUST retain control over them at all times including by not placing them in checked-in luggage or leaving them unattended.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Travelling personnel requested to decrypt mobile devices for inspection or from whom mobile devices are taken out of sight by border control MUST report the potential compromise of classified information or the device to an ITSM as soon as possible.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that when mobile devices are not being actively used they are secured in accordance with the minimum physical security requirements as stated in the PSR.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that the area within which mobile devices are used meets the minimum physical security requirements as stated in the PSR.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST undertake a risk assessment and implement appropriate controls BEFORE implementing a BYOD Policy and permitting the use of BYOD.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST take an integrated approach to BYOD security, covering policy, training, support, systems architecture, security, systems management, change management, incident detection & management and business continuity.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: BYOD MUST only be permitted for agency information systems up to and including RESTRICTED.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: BYOD MUST NOT be used for CONFIDENTIAL, SECRET or TOP SECRET systems.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Devices that have been “jail-broken”, “rooted” or have settings violations MUST NOT be used for any agency business or be allowed to connect to any agency systems UNLESS this been specifically authorised.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies may identify additional policy provisions and controls that are required, based on their assessment of risk.  Agencies MUST implement the additional controls and protocols before implementing BYOD.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST implement a BYOD acceptable use policy, agreed and signed by each person using a BYOD device.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The agency’s policy MUST clearly establish eligibility of personnel for participation in the agency BYOD scheme.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Personnel MUST have written authorisation (usually managerial approval) before a connection is enabled (on-boarding).

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Written authorisation MUST include the nature and extent of agency access approved, considering:

time, day of the week;
location; and
local or roaming access.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Procedures MUST be established for removal of agency installed software and any agency data when the user no longer has a need to use BYOD, is redeployed or ceases employment (off-boarding).

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Standard Operating Procedures for the agency’s BYOD network MUST be established.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provision MUST be made for contractors and other authorised non-employees.  It is at the agency’s discretion whether this activity is permitted.  The risk assessment MUST reflect this factor.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Ownership of data on BYOD devices MUST be clearly articulated and agreed.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agency policies MUST clearly articulate the separation between corporate support and where individuals are responsible for the maintenance and support of their own devices.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agency policies MUST clearly articulate the acceptable use of any GPS or other tracking capability.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Individual responsibility for the cost of any BYOD device and its accessories MUST be agreed.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Individual responsibility for replacement in the event of loss or theft MUST be agreed.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Individuals MUST be responsible for the installation and maintenance of any mandated BYOD-based firewalls and anti-malware software and for implementing operating system updates and patches on their device.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The procedures for purchasing and installing business related applications on the mobile devices MUST be specified and agreed.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The responsibility for payment of voice and data plans and roaming charges MUST be specified and agreed.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: A security architectural review MUST be undertaken by the agency before allowing BYOD devices to connect to agency systems.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The BYOD network segment MUST be segregated from other elements of the agency’s network.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST architecturally separate guest and public facing networks from BYOD networks.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Network configuration policies and authentication mechanisms MUST allow access to agency resources ONLY through the BYOD network segment.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access to internal resources and servers MUST be carefully managed and confined to only those services for which there is a defined and properly authorised business requirement.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Wireless access points used for access to agency networks MUST be implemented and secured in accordance with the directions in this manual (See Section 18.2 – Wireless Local Area Networks).

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Bluetooth on BYOD devices MUST be disabled while within designated secure areas on agency premises.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Access Controls MUST be implemented in accordance with Chapter 16 – Access Control.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST maintain a list of permitted operating systems, including operating system version numbers, for BYOD devices.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST check each BYOD device for malware and sanitise the device appropriately before installing agency software or operating environments.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST check each BYOD device for malware and sanitise the device appropriately before permitting access to agency data.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Intrusion detection systems MUST be implemented.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Continuous monitoring MUST be established to detect actual or potential security compromises or incidents from BYOD devices.  Refer also to Chapter 6 - Information Security Monitoring.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST maintain a list of approved cloud applications that may be used on BYOD devices.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST block the use of unapproved cloud applications for processing any agency or organisational data.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: BYOD devices MUST NOT be permitted direct connection to internal hosts, including all other devices on the local network.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: BYOD devices connecting to guest and public facing networks MUST NOT be permitted access to the corporate network other than through a VPN over the Internet.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Bluetooth on BYOD devices SHOULD be disabled while within agency premises and while accessing agency systems and data.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: BYOD devices and systems SHOULD use Multi-factor (at least two-factor) authentication to connect to agency systems and prior to being permitted access to agency data.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST implement a wireless IDS /IPS on BYOD wireless networks.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST implement rogue AP and wireless “hot spot” detection and implement response procedures where detection occurs.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose:
Agencies SHOULD conduct a baseline survey to identify:

All authorised devices and AP’s; and
Any unauthorised devices and AP’s.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any agency data exchanged with the mobile device MUST be encrypted in transit (See Chapter 17 – Cryptography).

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any agency data stored on the device MUST be encrypted (including keys, certificates and other essential session establishment data).

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The use of virtual containers, sandboxes, wraps or similar mechanisms on the mobile device MUST be established for each authorised session for any organisational data.  These mechanisms MUST be non-persistent and be removed at the end of each session.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Any sensitive agency data MUST be removed and securely deleted, or encrypted at the end of a session.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Connections to the agency network MUST be time limited to avoid leaving a session “logged on”.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Communications between the mobile device and the agency network MUST be established through a Virtual Private Network (VPN).

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST disable split-tunnelling when using a BYOD device to connect to an agency network (See Section 21.1 – Agency Owned Mobile Devices).

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST disable the ability for a BYOD device to establish simultaneous connections (e.g. wireless and cellular) when connected to an agency’s network.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: The use of passwords or PINs to unlock the BYOD device MUST be enforced in addition to all other agency authentication mechanisms.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: BYOD device passwords MUST be distinct from any agency access and authentication passwords.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: BYOD passwords MUST be distinct from other fixed or mobile agency network passwords (See Section 16.1 – Identification and Authentication for details on password requirements).

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD compile a list of approved BYOD devices and operating systems for the guidance of staff.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider the implementation of Data Loss Prevention (DLP) technologies.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD consider the use of bandwidth limits as a means of controlling data downloads and uploads.

controls.title: Non-Agency Owned Devices and Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD take legal advice on the provisions in their BYOD policy.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST clearly identify and understand where classification, security domain, and trust zone boundaries exist prior to implementation or adoption of public cloud services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where multiple identity systems under different security policies are used to control access to an agency’s instance of a public cloud service, the instance MUST be considered to be in a separate security domain from services where access control is managed solely by the agency’s identity system.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST clearly identify and understand their cloud service provider’s security responsibilities for each service consumed, and the aspects of security that the agency is responsible for, prior to implementation or adoption of the service.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD clearly document the aspects of security they and their provider are responsible for.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD review existing security processes to ensure compatibility with their cloud service provider’s responsibilities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD deploy and manage their cloud infrastructure using automation, version control, and infrastructure as code techniques where these are available.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST update their risk assessment process to account for public cloud specific risks, prior to implementation or adoption of public cloud services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST undertake a cloud specific risk assessment in line with the process outlined by the GCDO for each public cloud service, prior to implementation or adoption of public cloud services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT accredit public cloud services for use with data classified CONFIDENTIAL, SECRET, or TOP SECRET.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST NOT accredit public cloud services to host, process, store, or transmit NZEO endorsed information.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST consider risks to the availability of systems and information in their design of cloud systems architectures, supporting controls, and governance processes prior to implementation or adoption of public cloud services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD obtain regular assurance checks on cloud service providers, ensuring they have been undertaken by a suitably qualified assessor.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST obtain assurance that cloud service providers undertake appropriate software and operating system patching and maintenance.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST obtain assurance that technical protections exist to adequately isolate tenants.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD make use of the GCSB endorsed baseline security templates where applicable.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Accounts used to perform privileged actions SHOULD NOT be synchronised between environments.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where administration interfaces or portals are accessible from the internet, privileged accounts MUST be configured to use multiple factors of authentication.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Where cloud service interfaces or portals are accessible from the internet, user accounts SHOULD be configured to use multiple factors of authentication.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Staff offboarding processes MUST be updated to include removing all access to public cloud based services, prior to implementation or adoption of public cloud services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that relying parties continually verify the authenticity of their identity provider’s responses, through for example, cryptographic signing of authentication requests and responses.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that relying parties use all available information from the identity provider to inform access control decisions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: For each cloud service, agencies MUST ensure that the mechanisms used to protect data meet agency requirements.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST update key management plans to account for differences in public cloud before storing organisational data in a public cloud environment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure their key management plan includes provision for migrating data from the cloud environment where it was created.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST apply the principle of least privilege and configure service endpoints to restrict access to authorised parties.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST identify where data used in conjunction with a public cloud service is stored or processed, including any replicas or backups that may be created.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agency risk assessments of public cloud services MUST include any risks arising from data location. Any actions required to mitigate these risks must be identified and documented prior to implementation or adoption of public cloud services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST update their disaster recovery plans prior to storing or replicating data in public cloud services, to ensure these plans address any cloud-specific aspects of backup and recovery.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: When planning tests of disaster recovery processes in accordance with 6.4.6 Backup strategy, agencies MUST include tests of any cloud-specific data recovery processes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST have a defined exit strategy for each public cloud service they consume, including a process by which their data can be retrieved and erased from the cloud service as part of contract termination.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure all data they need to retain is retrieved from the cloud service provider prior to decommissioning.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST have assurance that no agency-owned data is retained on the cloud service being decommissioned.

controls.title: Logging and Alerting in Public Cloud

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST understand the range of logging capabilities provided by their cloud service providers and determine whether they are sufficient for agency needs.

controls.title: Logging and Alerting in Public Cloud

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that logs associated with public cloud services are collected, protected, and that their integrity can be confirmed in accordance with the agency’s documented logging requirements.

controls.title: Logging and Alerting in Public Cloud

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies MUST ensure that cloud service provider logs are incorporated into overall enterprise logging and alerting systems or procedures in a timely manner to detect information security incidents.

controls.title: Logging and Alerting in Public Cloud

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Agencies SHOULD ensure that tools and procedures used to detect potential information security incidents account for the public cloud services being consumed by the agency.
